answer:
**Charles Dunoyer treats the freedom (liberty) of agricultural industry directly in Chapter 17 ("VII. De la liberté de l'industrie agricole"), the fifth chapter of *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL II*.**

This chapter is dedicated to the topic, discussing obstacles to agricultural liberty such as taxes, regulations on land use, hunting rights, forestry codes, and more, while contrasting free agricultural practices in places like Flanders and England with restrictions in France.[^1][^2]

Earlier chapters provide context: Chapter 14 covers general conditions for industrial liberty,[^3] Chapter 15 applies these to industries acting on things (starting with commerce/transport),[^4] and Chapter 16 addresses manufacturing liberty.[^5] Agriculture is specifically covered in Chapter 17.

[^1]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL II*, ch.17, [translation](https://falsafa.corpus/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/17-vii-de-la-liberte-de-lindustrie-agricole-130/translation/).
[^2]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL II*, ch.17, ["On the liberty of the agricultural industry"](works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/wiki/17-vii-de-la-liberte-de-lindustrie-agricole-130.card.md), wiki card (key passage on farmer motivations and impediments like taxes and laziness).
[^3]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL II*, ch.14, [translation](https://falsafa.corpus/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/14-iv-des-conditions-auxquelles-toute-industrie-peut-/translation/).
[^4]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL II*, ch.15, [translation](https://falsafa.corpus/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/15-v-application-de-ces-moyens-de-liberte-aux-diverse/translation/).
[^5]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL II*, ch.16, [translation](https://falsafa.corpus/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/16-vi-de-la-liberte-de-lindustrie-manufacturiere/translation/).
